project_specification.project_goal: When a third-party alert webhook (Datadog / Grafana / CloudWatch / Sentry) is received for a tenant, CauseFlow ingests it, dedups it, runs Sonnet-powered AI triage to assign a severity, kicks off a multi-agent investigation (logs, metrics, change, code, infra, DB) that produces a hypothesis tree, proposes a remediation plan, waits for a human to approve it, then executes the plan against the customer's AWS account (ECS / EC2 / SSM / Lambda / PR / rollback) with full audit hashing, progress streamed live over SSE, and the resulting runbook written back to the tenant's Hindsight long-term memory.
project_specification.overview: CauseFlow is the AI SRE control plane for engineering teams. It is delivered as a modular monolith (Modlito) in a single Node.js 22 process: 15 Clean Architecture modules, 17 port interfaces, 31 ElectroDB entities on a single DynamoDB table, 4 SQS queues with 4 DLQs, Redis 7 for cache + pub/sub, an Alpine.js + Tailwind SPA served by Hono at `/dashboard`, and an embeddable widget bundle. Customers onboard via Clerk-authenticated signup, pick a Stripe plan, install the relay into their own VPC, and from then on the platform triages every alert, runs an investigation, and presents a remediation plan ready for human approval. The investigation pipeline routes cheap questions to Haiku (logs, metrics) and expensive synthesis to Sonnet, costing roughly $0.70 per investigation with per-agent Langfuse traces. The platform is deployed as a control-plane API plus an investigation ECS worker; the relay (a separate git submodule) lives in the customer's network and brokers DB / Mongo / log access back to the control plane over an authenticated WSS tunnel.

technology_stack.general:
Monorepo-style single project (this is the `core` project of the `causeflow-ai` workspace, alongside the `relay` git submodule registered separately in `.harness/projects.json`). Modular monolith deployed as a single Node.js 22 process with 15 Clean Architecture modules under `src/modules/`. Strict layer rule: Infra → Application → Domain; Domain is pure TypeScript types with zero external imports. Cross-module communication is restricted to (1) the in-process EventBus pub/sub and (2) type imports from another module's `domain/` only. Composition root is `src/bootstrap.ts`. pnpm workspace (`packageManager: pnpm@9.15.0`, enforced via corepack in Dockerfile). Two entry binaries: the API at `src/main.ts` (built to `dist/main.js`, run as `node dist/main.js`) and the investigation worker at `src/workers/investigation-worker.ts` (built to `dist/workers/investigation-worker.js`). Graceful shutdown is wired in `src/lifecycle.ts`. Conventional Commits; ESLint + Prettier; invariant linter (`pnpm lint-invariants` → `infra/scripts/check-invariants.ts`) enforces I1–I11.
Runtime and framework stack: Hono (4.x) with @hono/node-server (HTTP adapter that boots the API in src/main.ts) and @hono/zod-validator (request validation). TypeScript ^5.7. Structured JSON logging via pino (+ pino-pretty in dev). JWT verification via jose.
Persistence and cache: ElectroDB ^2.14 over a single DynamoDB table (31 entities, 3 GSIs, PITR). Redis 7 client via ioredis 5.4 (`src/shared/infra/cache/redis-client.ts`).
Queues and pub/sub: SQS Client / Consumer / DLQ Redriver (`src/shared/infra/queue/{sqs-client,sqs-consumer,sqs-message-queue,dlq-redriver}.ts`), an in-process EventBus (`src/shared/domain/events.ts`, consumed by `src/shared/infra/pubsub/progress-consumer.ts`), a Redis PubSub adapter (`src/shared/infra/pubsub/redis-pubsub.ts`), and an SQS Progress Publisher (`src/shared/infra/pubsub/sqs-progress.ts`).
AI and agent runners: PTC Anthropic Agent Runner (`src/shared/infra/llm/anthropic-ptc-agent-runner.ts` + `src/shared/infra/llm/enhanced-ptc-runner.ts`), a plain Anthropic Agent Runner that wraps the LLMClient port (`src/shared/infra/llm/anthropic-agent-runner.ts`), and a Mastra Agent Runner (`src/shared/infra/llm/mastra-agent-runner.ts` + `mastra-tool-adapter.ts`).
Cloud and credentials: a Stub Cloud Provider used in dev/test (`src/shared/infra/cloud/stub-cloud-provider.ts`), an AWS Cloud Provider (`src/shared/infra/cloud/aws-cloud-provider.ts`), an Azure Cloud Provider Stub placeholder (`src/shared/infra/cloud/azure-cloud-provider-stub.ts`). An STS Credential Vendor (`src/shared/infra/credentials/sts-credential-vendor.ts` + `role-policy-builder.ts`) with a Stub Credential Vendor for tests (`stub-credential-vendor.ts`) and KMS Token Encryption for stored customer tokens (`kms-token-encryption.ts`).
ECS dispatch and the Relay tunnel: an ECS Investigation Task Dispatcher (`src/shared/infra/ecs/ecs-client.ts` + `src/shared/infra/ecs/task-dispatcher.ts`) launches each investigation as an ECS task. The Relay WSS Gateway (`src/shared/infra/relay/relay-ws-server.ts` + `relay-registry.ts` + `relay-auth.ts` + `relay-protocol.ts` + `relay-gateway.ts`) accepts tunneled connections from the customer-side relay submodule.
Live streaming: an SSE Manager (`src/shared/infra/chat/sse-manager.ts`) for per-investigation progress; a cost tracking module (`src/shared/domain/cost.ts`) enforces a per-investigation ceiling.
HTTP middleware: Tenant middleware (`src/shared/infra/http/middleware/tenant.middleware.ts` + `rate-limit.middleware.ts`), Auth middleware (`auth.middleware.ts`), RBAC middleware (`rbac.middleware.ts` + `staff.middleware.ts`), Webhook HMAC middleware (`webhook-auth.middleware.ts`), Audit middleware (`audit.middleware.ts`), Request logger middleware (`request-logger.middleware.ts`), and Widget auth + CORS middleware (`widget-auth.middleware.ts` + `widget-cors.middleware.ts`).
Operations: a HealthChecker (`src/shared/infra/health/health-checker.ts` + per-dependency checks at `src/shared/infra/health/checks/{dynamodb-check,redis-check,sqs-check,anthropic-check}.ts`), an Invariant linter (`infra/scripts/check-invariants.ts` + `INVARIANTS.md`), and an In-process scheduler (`src/shared/infra/scheduler/scheduler.ts`).
Push: Web Push (VAPID) for end-user browser notifications; the `web-push` library is the runtime dep and the VAPID keys live in `.env.example`.
Observability: OpenTelemetry SDK (api, sdk-node, exporter-trace-otlp-proto, auto-instrumentations-node, AWS X-Ray id generator + propagator) initialised in `src/shared/infra/observability/otel.ts`.
Testing: Vitest (^2.1) as the runner; Promptfoo (^0.120) for LLM eval suites.

frontend.framework: Single-page Alpine.js + Tailwind application (`core/dashboard/index.html`) served by Hono at `/dashboard`; internal embeddable widget built with Vite at `core/packages/widget/`
frontend.styling: Tailwind CSS (bundled in the dashboard HTML and in the widget Vite build); no separate component library — small set of utility-classed Alpine components
frontend.state_management: Alpine.js reactive stores + Hono SSE for live progress streams; no Redux/MobX/Vuex
frontend.routing: Client-side hash routing in the dashboard SPA; Hono server routes serve JSON APIs and the single `index.html` shell
frontend.port: Launch the dashboard and widget on the same PORT env var as the API; dashboard served at `/dashboard`, widget bundle served from `/widget/`, widget embed endpoint at `/embed/
frontend.patterns: Single-file Alpine SPA — no per-component build step. Widget is a Vite library build consumed via script tag.

backend.database: Single DynamoDB table with ElectroDB ^2.14; 31 entities; 3 GSIs; PITR enabled. Local replacement: nahuelnucera/ministack on :4566. See `database_schema` for the full list.
backend.port: Launch the API on the PORT env var (default 3099); investigation worker runs as a separate `node dist/workers/investigation-worker.js` process. The control plane also exposes `/v1/relay/connect` (WSS) for the relay gateway on the same port.
backend.patterns: Clean Architecture (Domain / Application / Infra) per module, dependency rule Infra → Application → Domain, ports & adapters with 17 port interfaces in `src/shared/application/ports/`, EventBus for cross-module events, composition root at `src/bootstrap.ts`. Strict invariants: I1–I11 enforced by `pnpm lint-invariants`.

technology_stack.communication: REST/JSON over Hono for all `/api/v1/...` routes. SSE (Hono streaming) for live progress on investigations, triage and remediation (`src/shared/infra/chat/sse-manager.ts`). WSS gateway at `/v1/relay/connect` for relay tunnels (`src/shared/infra/relay/relay-ws-server.ts`). Internal cross-module events via the in-process EventBus (`src/shared/domain/event-bus.ts`). SQS for durable async work (4 queues + 4 DLQs in `infra/localstack/init/01-create-resources.sh`); Redis pub/sub (`src/shared/infra/pubsub/redis-pubsub.ts`) for low-latency progress fan-out; an SQS progress publisher (`src/shared/infra/pubsub/sqs-progress.ts`) for durable replay. Outbound: Clerk JWT verification, Stripe webhooks, Composio triggers + tools, Slack Web API, Svix for outbound webhooks, Sentry for error capture, Langfuse for AI traces, Hindsight for long-term agent memory.

integration.name: AWS SDK v3 (DynamoDB / DocumentClient)
integration.responsibility: Persistence layer: every entity read/write goes through ElectroDB over @aws-sdk/client-dynamodb and @aws-sdk/lib-dynamodb. Single-table design with 3 GSIs; PITR enabled.
integration.configuration: AWS_REGION, AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID, AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY (or IAM role in prod). Table name via env (causeflow local table created by `infra/localstack/init/01-create-resources.sh`). Endpoint override (DYNAMODB_ENDPOINT) for ministack dev.
integration.security: IAM role per environment; tenant isolation enforced at the application layer via composite SK prefixes; per-tenant key scoping inside every ElectroDB query. No cross-tenant queries possible without a deliberate PK rewrite.
integration.failure_behavior: Retries with exponential backoff via the AWS SDK defaults. Health check at `/health` via `src/shared/infra/health/checks/dynamodb-check.ts`. Tables not yet ready surface as 503.
integration.deployment: Production: real DynamoDB on the customer's account or CauseFlow's account depending on tenant model. Local: nahuelnucera/ministack on :4566, initialized by the localstack init script.
integration.affected_surfaces: All 15 modules (every module has at least one repository)

integration.name: AWS SDK v3 (SQS / STS / KMS)
integration.responsibility: SQS: durable async queues (4 queues + 4 DLQs for alerts / triage / investigation / remediation + their DLQs). STS: assume-role for cross-account credential vending. KMS: envelope encryption for stored customer tokens at rest.
integration.configuration: Queue URLs and ARNs wired in bootstrap; KMS alias `alias/causeflow-token-encryption` created by the localstack init script. STS roles are provisioned per tenant on connect.
integration.security: STS session policies restricted to the tenant's account and the actions needed for remediation; KMS grants scoped to the alias and the control-plane role. Token ciphertext stored in DynamoDB; never plaintext.
integration.failure_behavior: SQS retries with visibility timeout + DLQ; DLQ redriver at `src/shared/infra/queue/dlq-redriver.ts` for ops to replay poison messages. KMS failures surface as 5xx on the affected API route.
integration.affected_surfaces: Ingestion (alerts), Triage, Investigation (worker pulls), Remediation (worker pulls), Credentials (STS + KMS).

integration.name: AWS SDK v3 (CloudWatch / CloudWatch Logs / CloudTrail)
integration.responsibility: CloudWatch Logs: the AWS provider pulls log streams for the investigation log agent. CloudWatch Metrics: the metrics agent queries customer-side metrics. CloudTrail: change-event correlation during investigations.
integration.configuration: Endpoint override (CW_ENDPOINT) for local dev; region + role assumption. Log group patterns configurable per tenant.
integration.security: Access is mediated through the STS credential vendor so the control plane only ever assumes the customer-managed role with session policy.
integration.failure_behavior: Per-call retry with circuit breaker (`src/shared/infra/llm/circuit-breaker.ts` re-used for AWS calls). Health check included via the Anthropic-style checks pattern.
integration.deployment: Production: real CloudWatch via assumed role. Local: ministack returns canned responses.
integration.affected_surfaces: Investigation (log + metric + change agents), Remediation (verifies rollback outcomes).

integration.name: AWS SDK v3 (ECS / EC2 / EKS / ELBv2 / ElastiCache)
integration.responsibility: ECS: investigation task dispatcher (`src/shared/infra/ecs/task-dispatcher.ts`) runs each investigation as an ECS task. EC2 / EKS: list, inspect, restart. ELBv2: target group re-drain. ElastiCache: cache state read.
integration.availability: required for investigation + remediation; some clients only used for inspection
integration.configuration: Region, assumed role, ECS cluster + task definition ARNs. Task definition is built by `infra/cdk/lib/causeflow-stack.ts`.
integration.security: All calls go through the per-tenant STS role; no static credentials in the control plane.
integration.failure_behavior: ECS task failures (exit non-zero) raise an investigation failure event; remediation rollbacks re-run on the next attempt.
integration.deployment: Production: real ECS cluster on CauseFlow's account. Local: stubbed via `src/shared/infra/cloud/stub-cloud-provider.ts`.
integration.affected_surfaces: Investigation (dispatcher), Remediation (execute on ECS/EC2/EKS), Cloud provider port (`src/shared/infra/cloud/aws-cloud-provider.ts`).

integration.name: AWS SDK v3 (Lambda / SSM / EventBridge / SNS / RDS / Route53 / S3 / ApiGateway / ApiGatewayV2 / CloudFront / CodeCommit)
integration.responsibility: Remediation executors: invoke a Lambda, run an SSM document, publish an EventBridge event, send an SNS notification, restart an RDS instance, change a Route53 record, roll back an S3 object, redeploy via API Gateway / CloudFront, open a CodeCommit PR.
integration.availability: required for remediation actions; some are feature-flagged per tenant
integration.configuration: Region + assumed role for each executor; per-action toggles in the tenant settings.
integration.security: Per-tenant assumed role with action-scoped session policy. Each action is previewed and human-approved before execution.
integration.failure_behavior: Per-action retry; failure raises a remediation_failed event and rolls back where possible. Each action logged in the audit chain.
integration.deployment: Production: real AWS accounts. Local: stub cloud provider returns deterministic mock results.
integration.affected_surfaces: Remediation module executors, Cloud provider port.

integration.name: Clerk
integration.responsibility: Authentication: signup, sign-in, session, organization (tenant) management, JWT issuance. Verifies JWTs on every authenticated Hono route.
integration.configuration: CLERK_SECRET_KEY, CLERK_PUBLISHABLE_KEY, CLERK_JWT_KEY (configured in `src/shared/config/index.ts`). Webhook signing secret for user.created / user.updated events.
integration.security: Bearer JWTs validated by `src/shared/infra/http/middleware/auth.middleware.ts`; tenant extracted via `src/shared/infra/http/middleware/tenant.middleware.ts`; RBAC via `rbac.middleware.ts` + `staff.middleware.ts`.
integration.failure_behavior: Missing / invalid / expired token → 401. Stale Clerk user → re-sync on next request.
integration.deployment: Production: real Clerk. Local: dev Clerk instance (the .env.localstack disables Clerk).
integration.affected_surfaces: Auth module, all authenticated Hono routes, webhook ingest for user lifecycle.

integration.name: Stripe
integration.responsibility: Billing: plan selection on signup, metered usage, subscription state, invoice webhooks, dunning.
integration.configuration: STRIPE_SECRET_KEY, STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET, STRIPE_PRICE_* IDs. Webhook endpoint at `/api/v1/billing/webhook` verified by signature.
integration.security: Webhook signature verification (HMAC) at the route layer; never trust client-supplied price IDs.
integration.failure_behavior: Webhook delivery retries automatically; signature failure → 400; missing customer → 404. Usage records reconciled nightly.
integration.deployment: Production: real Stripe. Local / E2E: stripe-mock or test mode keys in `.env.e2e`.
integration.affected_surfaces: Billing module (`src/modules/billing/`), tenant signup flow, plan-gated features.

integration.name: Composio
integration.responsibility: Third-party tool + trigger layer: Composio clients (GitHub, Linear, Notion, PagerDuty, etc.) wired as triggers and tools available to the investigation and remediation agents.
integration.availability: required for non-AWS tooling (GitHub, Linear, PagerDuty, Notion)
integration.configuration: COMPOSIO_API_KEY; per-user OAuth handled by Composio's hosted flow. Tool selection via trigger IDs at `src/shared/infra/integrations/composio-tool-provider.ts`.
integration.security: Per-tenant OAuth tokens stored encrypted via KMS token encryption; Composio client (`composio-client.ts`) is the only call site.
integration.failure_behavior: Tool call failures bubble back to the agent as observed tool errors; trigger subscription failures retry with backoff.
integration.deployment: Production: real Composio. Local: stubbed via test fixtures in `tests/fixtures/`.
integration.affected_surfaces: Integration module (`src/modules/integration/`), investigation agents, remediation executors.

integration.name: Slack Web API
integration.responsibility: Two-way chat with on-call: the investigation chat panel can post updates and thread replies into a Slack channel, and the user can drive the investigation from Slack via slash-style commands.
integration.configuration: SLACK_BOT_TOKEN, SLACK_SIGNING_SECRET; OAuth state in `SlackOAuthStateEntity.ts`; per-tenant settings store the channel mapping.
integration.security: Slack signing secret used to verify inbound events; bot token scoped to the channels the user authorized.
integration.failure_behavior: Slack API failures don't block investigations — they degrade the chat surface and log a Sentry error.
integration.deployment: Production: real Slack workspace. Local: `stub-chat-platform.ts` for tests; manual install via `src/modules/integration/infra/slack.routes.ts`.
integration.affected_surfaces: Integration module (slack routes), chat platform port, notification module.

integration.name: Svix
integration.responsibility: Outbound webhook delivery to customer-configured HTTP endpoints (e.g. "investigation.completed", "remediation.executed"). Handles retries, signing, and delivery guarantees.
integration.configuration: SVIX_API_KEY, SVIX_APP_ID; endpoints registered per tenant in `IntegrationEntity.ts`.
integration.security: Each outbound webhook signed with a per-endpoint HMAC secret; recipients verify via Svix-standard headers.
integration.failure_behavior: Retries with exponential backoff managed by Svix; failed deliveries surface in the audit module.
integration.deployment: Production: real Svix. Local / E2E: Svix local (in docker-compose).
integration.affected_surfaces: Integration module (webhook dispatch), audit module (delivery status).

integration.name: Sentry
integration.responsibility: Error and exception capture across the API and worker. PII stripped before send.
integration.security: PII scrubbing enabled; source maps uploaded only for tagged releases; never logs raw tokens or customer data.
integration.failure_behavior: Send failures swallowed silently so observability never takes down the request path.
integration.deployment: Production: real Sentry project. Local: SENTRY_DSN empty → no-op.
integration.affected_surfaces: API, worker, both error handlers and the global error middleware.

integration.name: Langfuse
integration.responsibility: AI observability: per-agent traces (prompts, completions, tool calls, token counts, cost, latency) are recorded against the investigation ID. Includes a metric recorder for the cost ceiling.
integration.configuration: LANGFUSE_PUBLIC_KEY, LANGFUSE_SECRET_KEY, LANGFUSE_BASE_URL (local: http://localhost:3001 in docker-compose). Tracer at `src/shared/infra/observability/langfuse-tracer.ts`, metric recorder at `langfuse-metric-recorder.ts`. Mastra integration via `@mastra/langfuse`.
integration.security: API keys scoped to the CauseFlow org in Langfuse; tenant ID passed as a Langfuse tag so traces are filterable per tenant.
integration.failure_behavior: Trace send failures are buffered and dropped after a TTL — never block an agent call.
integration.deployment: Production: Langfuse Cloud or self-hosted. Local: langfuse + postgres in docker-compose.
integration.affected_surfaces: All agents (log / metric / change / code / infra / DB analysts + synthesis), cost tracking.

integration.name: Hindsight
integration.responsibility: Long-term agent memory. After every investigation, the resolved hypothesis + runbook is written to the tenant's Hindsight bank. New investigations query the bank for similar past incidents.
integration.configuration: HINDSIGHT_API_URL (local: http://localhost:8888), HINDSIGHT_API_KEY. Adapter at `src/shared/infra/memory/hindsight-agent-memory.ts`; docker-compose also spins up the `hindsight` container (ghcr.io/vectorize-io/hindsight:latest).
integration.security: Per-tenant bank; the bank ID is derived from the tenant ID so cross-tenant recall is impossible at the data layer.
integration.failure_behavior: Memory writes are best-effort post-success — failure does not fail the investigation. Recall failures fall back to "no prior context".
integration.deployment: Production: Hindsight Cloud (or self-hosted). Local: vectorize-io/hindsight container.
integration.affected_surfaces: Memory module, investigation post-success hook, code-intelligence module (recall for similar code patterns).

integration.name: Anthropic API
integration.responsibility: Claude model access. Two SDKs are used: `@anthropic-ai/sdk` for direct calls and `@ai-sdk/anthropic` for the agent / streaming use cases. The Anthropic client is at `src/shared/infra/llm/anthropic-client.ts`.
integration.configuration: ANTHROPIC_API_KEY. Model slots: ANTHROPIC_TRIAGE_MODEL, ANTHROPIC_INVESTIGATION_MODEL, ANTHROPIC_SYNTHESIS_MODEL, ANTHROPIC_SEEKER_MODEL, ANTHROPIC_INFRA_INSPECTOR_MODEL, AGENT_MODEL_CODE_FIXER, ANTHROPIC_DB_ANALYST_MODEL, ANTHROPIC_ORCHESTRATOR_MODEL, ANTHROPIC_FOLLOWUP_MODEL. Defaults: claude-sonnet-4-6 in `src/shared/config/index.ts` (config wins — .env.example is pinned to an older snapshot).
integration.security: API key from env / secret store; no per-tenant keys. All calls traced through Langfuse with tenant tag.
integration.failure_behavior: Circuit breaker (`src/shared/infra/llm/circuit-breaker.ts`) opens after repeated failures; observability via the Anthropic health check at `src/shared/infra/health/checks/anthropic-check.ts`.
integration.deployment: Production: real Anthropic API. Local: real API key in .env.dev; tests stub the LLM client port.
integration.affected_surfaces: All agents, triage, code fixer, synthesis, follow-up.

integration.name: Mastra
integration.responsibility: Optional agent framework. When enabled, the Mastra agent runner (`src/shared/infra/llm/mastra-agent-runner.ts`) wraps Claude calls in Mastra's tool-calling + memory primitives. Integrates with @mastra/dynamodb, @mastra/langfuse, @mastra/memory, @mastra/observability.
integration.configuration: MASTRA_ENABLED flag; otherwise the Anthropic agent runner is used directly.
integration.security: Same auth surface as Anthropic API; Mastra tools go through the Composio tool provider.
integration.failure_behavior: Runner-level retries; failure falls back to the Anthropic agent runner when the flag is off.
integration.deployment: Production: same as Anthropic. Local: in-process; no separate deployment.

integrations.contradictions: The causeflow-core documentation drifts from the implementation in five known places. The implementation is treated as authoritative, and the spec states the actual numbers / values: (1) 31 ElectroDB entities in the spec; docs are stale — CLAUDE.md and README both still say 28. (2) 17 ports in the spec; docs are stale — CLAUDE.md says 16. (3) config wins (current implementation) — claude-sonnet-4-6 is the live default; .env.example is a pinned older snapshot (says claude-sonnet-4-5-20250929). (4) config wins for runtime default (sa-east-1); .envrc is the per-developer shell override; .env.example is documentation (concretely: config wins for runtime default (sa-east-1); .envrc is the per-developer shell override; .env.example is documentation, and the documented AWS region variants are us-east-1 in .env.example, us-east-2 in .envrc, sa-east-1 in src/shared/config/index.ts). (5) ministack is the actual local image — docker-compose.yml uses nahuelnucera/ministack, not vanilla localstack; the README/CLAUDE.md still call it "LocalStack". Concretely: ministack is the actual local image; document names diverged from implementation (the docs say "LocalStack" but the image and the env var name are both "ministack").

core_features.foundation: - docker-compose up -d brings up ministack, redis, langfuse, hindsight and the customer sample services; the localstack init script creates the table + queues + DLQs + KMS alias - pnpm dev starts the Hono API on PORT (default 3099) and the dashboard is served at /dashboard; widget bundle at /widget/ - The /health endpoint reports DynamoDB, Redis, SQS and Anthropic reachability via HealthChecker - 31 ElectroDB entities (more than the 28 in the docs) live in src/shared/infra/db/entities/ and are registered through a single DynamoDB table with 3 GSIs and PITR - 17 application ports in src/shared/application/ports/ implement the dependency-inversion boundary (LLM, queue, cache, cloud, credentials, memory, chat, integration, relay, observability, tracer, metric, agent, token-encryption, code-repository, alert-source, trigger) - The composition root src/bootstrap.ts is the only place concrete implementations are wired to abstractions - pnpm lint-invariants enforces I1–I11 (no cross-module direct calls, no infra-in-domain, no hard-coded secrets, etc.) - A per-investigation cost ceiling is enforced via src/shared/domain/cost.ts; Langfuse records the spend per agent - Graceful shutdown of the API and worker is implemented in src/lifecycle.ts (drains in-flight requests, drains SQS consumers, flushes observability buffers)
core_features.tenancy-and-access: - Tenant model: every entity carries a tenantId PK prefix; tenant middleware sets the request context from the JWT and the path - Clerk is the identity provider; signup creates a tenant and a billing account in one transaction - RBAC roles: owner, admin, member, viewer; staff (CauseFlow employees) get a parallel role checked by staff.middleware.ts - API keys for programmatic access are issued by api-key.routes.ts and scoped per-tenant - Beta allowlist gates tenants into feature flags via beta-allowlist.routes.ts - Audit middleware hashes every state-changing request into the audit hash chain; audit.routes.ts exposes the chain for review - User settings (timezone, notification preferences, theme) stored in UserSettingsEntity - Invites: an owner can invite by email; InviteEntity tracks pending invites
core_features.billing: - Stripe plan selection on signup (free / pro / enterprise) - Metered usage records persisted in UsageRecordEntity (per-investigation, per-remediation, per-agent-call) - Stripe webhook endpoint at /api/v1/billing/webhook (signature-verified) - Invoice list, current subscription, and plan-change endpoints under /api/v1/billing/* - Dunning and grace period handled in the application layer - Per-tenant quota enforcement (max parallel investigations, max agents per run)
core_features.ingestion: - Webhook parsers for Datadog, Grafana, CloudWatch and Sentry, each with HMAC signature verification (webhook-auth.middleware.ts) - Manual incident creation endpoint for human-triggered reports - Admin endpoints for re-running ingestion and replaying failed webhooks - Analytics endpoints: alert volume, MTTA, MTTR per tenant - Dedup: identical (source, fingerprint) alerts within a configurable window collapse into a single IncidentEntity - Deduplicated alerts are enqueued to the triage SQS queue
core_features.triage: - AI severity classification: Sonnet model (ANTHROPIC_TRIAGE_MODEL, default claude-sonnet-4-6) reads the alert payload + tenant context and returns severity + suggested investigation mode - Triaged incident entity stored in IncidentEntity with status transitions: received → triaged → investigating → resolved - Triage routes expose the triaged list and per-incident detail at /api/v1/triage/* - Backfill script `pnpm backfill:stranded-triage` recovers any incidents stuck pre-triage - Langfuse traces per triage decision for cost and quality analysis
core_features.investigation: - Three modes: orchestrator (default), hypothesis, debate - Six or more agents run in parallel: log agent (Haiku), metric agent (Haiku), change agent, code agent, infra inspector, DB analyst, plus a synthesis agent - Each agent emits evidence (EvidenceEntity), hypotheses (HypothesisEntity), and findings - The orchestrator manages the iteration loop, deduplicates overlapping evidence, and prunes low-confidence hypotheses - A live progress stream is exposed over SSE so the dashboard shows per-agent updates in real time - A registry of past runs (per tenant) is queryable; runs are filterable by status, severity, time window - An investigation chat panel lets the user steer the investigation; messages are stored in ChatMessageEntity - Investigation routes under /api/v1/investigation/* expose list, detail, retry, cancel, and chat
core_features.remediation: - A remediation is proposed at the end of an investigation; the proposal lists the AWS actions to take - A human (with owner/admin RBAC) approves the plan; the approval is hashed into the audit chain (ApprovalEntity) - The plan is executed against the customer's AWS account through the cloud provider port: ECS / EC2 / SSM / Lambda / Route53 / RDS / S3 / API Gateway / CloudFront / CodeCommit PR - Each action is logged in RemediationEntity with before/after state - Rollback action is proposed automatically for mutating actions and runnable in one click - The user can pin a remediation to a runbook (RunbookRegistryEntity) and a future similar incident will surface the same remediation
core_features.memory-and-skills: - Hindsight bank per tenant; bank ID derived from the tenant ID - Successful investigations + the runbook they produced are written to the bank - New investigations query the bank for similar past incidents and surface the top-k runbooks to the orchestrator - Per-tenant skills (SkillEntity) teach the agents the customer's runbooks, escalation paths, and naming conventions - Chat history per investigation is persisted and queryable for follow-up - Recall failures fall back to "no prior context" and never fail the investigation
core_features.code-intelligence: - The customer's repositories are indexed once and stored as RepoNodeEntity + ServiceEdgeEntity + RepoServiceMapEntity - PackageDependencyEntity captures the dependency graph across repos - The code agent uses the index to answer "which service owns this log line?" and "what depends on this package?" - PatternEntity holds recurring code patterns (e.g. "retry-with-backoff") discovered across the tenant's repos - A code search endpoint /api/v1/code-knowledge/* accepts a natural-language query and returns the matching repos + lines - The code agent (Sonnet) proposes patches; the patch is opened as a CodeCommit PR through the remediation flow
core_features.integrations-and-notifications: - GitHub App for connecting the customer's repositories (fallback for CodeCommit) - Composio wraps 20+ third-party tools (Linear, Notion, PagerDuty, etc.) and exposes them to the agents - Slack integration for two-way chat (oauth + signing secret + channel mapping in SlackOAuthStateEntity / SlackNotificationEntity) - Sentry integration for ingesting Sentry alerts as incidents - Relay routes accept tunneled requests from the customer's relay (DB / Mongo / log access) — relay.registry, relay.protocol, relay.auth at /v1/relay/* - Web Push notifications via VAPID; per-user subscription stored in NotificationEntity - Progress events for any tenant operation can be streamed over SSE
core_features.widget-and-portal: - The embeddable widget (packages/widget) is a Vite library build shipped as a single JS file - A tenant can drop a <script> tag into their status page or internal tool to mount the widget - The widget calls the control plane over a tenant-scoped widget session token (WidgetSessionEntity) - The portal at /embed/ gives the tenant's end-users read-only visibility into the latest investigation - The dashboard SPA at /dashboard (Alpine.js + Tailwind, served by Hono) gives the tenant's engineers the full control plane
core_features.observability-and-ops: - Langfuse tracer at src/shared/infra/observability/langfuse-tracer.ts records per-agent prompts / completions / tool calls / cost / latency - OpenTelemetry SDK initialised in src/shared/infra/observability/otel.ts; traces exported to OTLP and correlated with AWS X-Ray via the X-Ray id generator - Sentry captures uncaught exceptions across the API and worker - HealthChecker at /health reports DynamoDB / Redis / SQS / Anthropic reachability - In-process scheduler runs periodic jobs (DLQ redrive, cost rollup, Hindsight bank compaction, usage reconciliation) — no separate cron - Cost tracking enforces a per-investigation ceiling; the metric recorder publishes to Langfuse and to the audit module
core_features.open-source-local-runtime: - Goal: the `core` service runs end-to-end via `docker compose up` with zero paid/external SaaS credentials configured. Only Hindsight (and an optional Anthropic key) are required to use the product. - Persistence: Postgres 16 replaces DynamoDB. Every entity in the existing 31-entity set is ported to a Postgres table in a dedicated `causeflow` schema inside the bundled `causeflow-postgres` container. Repository interfaces in `domain/` are unchanged; only the `infra/` implementations swap from ElectroDB to a thin SQL layer (one table per entity, composite primary key `(tenant_id, entity_type, entity_id)`). - Queue: BullMQ on the existing Redis 7 replaces SQS. The `MessageQueuePort` keeps the same `enqueue` / `subscribe` shape; the `SqsMessageQueue` is replaced by `BullMqMessageQueue`. A small Bull Board / `GET /admin/queues` endpoint is exposed for ops visibility. Retry + DLQ semantics are preserved. - Auth: local username/password + JWT replaces Clerk. `POST /api/v1/auth/register` creates a tenant + owner user in one transaction; `POST /api/v1/auth/login` issues a short-lived JWT signed with a server secret; the existing `auth.middleware.ts` continues to verify, but no longer calls `@clerk/backend`. The Clerk webhook endpoint is removed; invites become a simple emailed link (or a JSON token when SMTP is not configured). - Billing: a `StubBillingService` replaces Stripe. Every tenant is on the "free" plan by default; the only configuration is per-tenant quota overrides via env or admin endpoint. The `/api/v1/billing/*` routes return canned data and the webhook endpoint is removed. `UsageRecordEntity` keeps tracking metered usage, but no external meter is called. - Credentials: the `CredentialVendorPort` is implemented by `EnvCredentialVendor` — credentials come from `.env` (`CAUSEFLOW_TENANT_<id>_AWS_*` style or a JSON blob), no STS AssumeRole, no cross-account policy builder. The `StsCredentialVendor` and `role-policy-builder` are removed. - Token encryption: `AesGcmTokenEncryption` (Node `crypto` AES-256-GCM) replaces KMS. The encryption key is loaded from `TOKEN_ENCRYPTION_KEY` env var. The `KmsTokenEncryption` and the localstack init script for the KMS alias are removed. - Task dispatch: the existing `investigation-worker` process (already a separate `node dist/workers/investigation-worker.js` binary) runs as a docker-compose service. `EcsInvestigationTaskDispatcher` is replaced by `LocalInvestigationTaskDispatcher` which spawns the worker in the same docker network. No ECS task definition, no AWS account required. - Cloud provider: `StubCloudProvider` is the only cloud provider. `AwsCloudProvider` and `AzureCloudProviderStub` are removed. The remediation flow runs against a deterministic mock that records every action in the audit chain; the dashboard shows the simulated before/after. - Optional SaaS are no-op when unconfigured: Sentry, Langfuse, Svix, Slack, Composio, Mastra. Each integration is gated on its env var being set; with an empty value, calls are swallowed and the relevant port returns a deterministic empty result. No SDK is invoked at startup. - Hindsight remains the one required external SaaS: writes + recall go to the bundled `ghcr.io/vectorize-io/hindsight` container (unchanged from the original spec). - `docker compose up -d` brings up: `causeflow-postgres`, `redis`, `hindsight`, the `causeflow-api` and `causeflow-worker` services, plus the customer sample services (order, payment, billing, notification, marketplace) for the demo. No `ministack`, no `langfuse`, no `localstack-init` script. `.env.example` is reduced to a short file with only Hindsight + Anthropic keys. - The 38 original acceptance checks still pass against the local-only stack (regression): webhook → ingest → dedup → triage → investigation → remediation → memory works without AWS, Stripe or Clerk.

acceptance_check.description: Run `docker-compose up -d`; within 60 seconds the ministack, redis, langfuse and hindsight containers report healthy. The init script `infra/localstack/init/01-create-resources.sh` has created the causeflow DynamoDB table, 4 SQS queues, 4 DLQs and the KMS alias `alias/causeflow-token-encryption`. A `curl http://localhost:4566/_ministack/health` returns 200.

Run `pnpm dev`; within 10 seconds the Hono API is listening on PORT (default 3099). A `curl http://localhost:3099/health` returns 200 with a JSON body listing {dynamodb: ok, redis: ok, sqs: ok, anthropic: ok} (Anthropic check is skipped when no key is configured).

A `curl http://localhost:3099/dashboard` returns the Alpine.js SPA shell HTML (Alpine + Tailwind present, an x-data root element). A `curl http://localhost:3099/widget/widget.js` returns the Vite-built widget bundle with a 200 Content-Type of application/javascript.

Run `pnpm lint-invariants` (which calls `infra/scripts/check-invariants.ts`); the command exits 0. Manually introducing a cross-module direct function call (e.g. importing a use case from another module's `application/`) and re-running `pnpm lint-invariants` makes the command exit non-zero with a specific I1–I11 violation message.

The DynamoDB table `causeflow-local` exists with 3 GSIs and Point-in-Time Recovery enabled. The list of tables via `aws --endpoint-url http://localhost:4566 dynamodb list-tables` includes `causeflow-local`; `aws --endpoint-url http://localhost:4566 dynamodb describe-table` shows `PointInTimeRecoveryDescription.PointInTimeRecoveryStatus = ENABLED` and 3 GlobalSecondaryIndexes.

The four SQS queues (alerts / triage / investigation / remediation) and their four matching DLQs exist on ministack; the KMS alias `alias/causeflow-token-encryption` exists. `aws --endpoint-url http://localhost:4566 sqs list-queues` returns 8 URLs and `aws --endpoint-url http://localhost:4566 kms list-aliases` returns an alias whose name ends with `alias/causeflow-token-encryption`.

POST a valid Clerk session JWT to /api/v1/auth/login. The auth middleware extracts the user, the tenant middleware extracts the tenant from the path, and the response contains the user, the tenant, and the user's role. A second call with no Authorization header returns 401.

As a `viewer`, GET /api/v1/audit returns 200; DELETE /api/v1/audit/:id returns 403 (RBAC). As an `admin`, the same DELETE returns 200 and the audit chain advances (the new entry's previousHash matches the prior tip).

POST /api/v1/api-keys with a name and scopes returns a plaintext API key on creation; subsequent calls to /api/v1/whoami with `Authorization: Bearer <key>` resolve the user and tenant. A second POST that would exceed the per-tenant key quota returns 429.

Two tenants A and B exist. Tenant A calls GET /api/v1/incident/abc123 and gets 200 for incident abc123 (owned by A). Tenant B calls the same URL and gets 404 (the incident is invisible). Direct PK construction across tenants is rejected at the application layer.

Stripe test mode: POST /api/v1/billing/checkout-session with a valid price ID returns a Stripe checkout URL. The Stripe CLI's `stripe trigger checkout.session.completed` posts a webhook; the webhook endpoint validates the signature, creates a BillingAccountEntity for the user, and the response is 200.

After a successful investigation completes, a UsageRecordEntity is written with the investigation ID, the per-agent token counts and the per-agent cost. GET /api/v1/billing/usage returns a paginated list of usage records scoped to the calling tenant only.

Stripe webhook with an invalid signature (mutated body) returns 400; valid signature returns 200. After `customer.subscription.deleted` arrives, the tenant's plan drops to "free" and gated endpoints return 402.

POST a Datadog-shaped payload to /api/v1/webhooks/datadog with a valid HMAC header; the webhook-auth middleware verifies the signature, the parser extracts the alert, and the incident is persisted in IncidentEntity with status=received. A second POST with an invalid signature returns 401.

POST the same Datadog payload twice within the dedup window; only one IncidentEntity is created (the second call returns the existing incident ID and does not enqueue a duplicate triage message). After the dedup window elapses, a third identical POST creates a new incident.

POST Grafana-, CloudWatch-, and Sentry-shaped payloads to /api/v1/webhooks/{grafana,cloudwatch,sentry}; each is parsed into an IncidentEntity and the parser-specific fields land in the right columns. POST /api/v1/admin/incidents with a manual payload creates an incident tagged source=manual.

After an incident is ingested, within 30 seconds the triage worker pulls the message, calls the Anthropic API with ANTHROPIC_TRIAGE_MODEL (default claude-sonnet-4-6), and updates the incident with a severity (one of critical|high|medium|low) and a suggested investigation mode. GET /api/v1/triage/:id returns the triaged incident with the severity set; GET /api/v1/triage lists triaged incidents for the tenant.

A Langfuse trace for the triage call exists in the local Langfuse UI at http://localhost:3001, tagged with the tenant ID and incident ID, and the trace contains the prompt, completion, token counts and cost. A trace is also recorded in the trace's `output` for the incident's audit chain.

Once an incident is triaged, the investigation worker starts a run with at least 6 agents (log, metric, change, code, infra, db). The investigation routes return a run with status=running; the SSE stream at /api/v1/investigation/:id/stream emits per-agent events as they complete. The run finishes with status=succeeded and the final synthesis is stored.

The finished investigation has at least one HypothesisEntity and at least one EvidenceEntity per active agent. GET /api/v1/investigation/:id/evidence returns the evidence list grouped by agent, with confidence scores. GET /api/v1/investigation/:id/hypotheses returns the hypothesis tree with parent/child links.

POST /api/v1/investigation/:id/chat with a follow-up question routes to the chat platform port, persists a ChatMessageEntity, and returns the agent's reply. Slack-equivalent: the same chat is mirrored to the connected Slack channel (when Slack is installed) and the Slack thread ID is stored on the message.

Switching ANTHROPIC_TRIAGE_MODEL or ANTHROPIC_INVESTIGATION_MODEL via .env changes the model used on the next run; the Langfuse trace shows the new model name. ANTHROPIC_API_KEY=invalid causes the Anthropic health check to flip to `degraded` and the investigation run to fail with a clear circuit-breaker error.

After a successful investigation, GET /api/v1/remediation/:id/proposal returns a plan listing the AWS actions to take. POST /api/v1/remediation/:id/approve with an admin JWT returns 200 and creates an ApprovalEntity. A second approve on the same remediation returns 409 (already approved).

The approved plan runs against the stub cloud provider. RemediationEntity records each action with status=succeeded and a before/after diff. POST /api/v1/remediation/:id/rollback creates a follow-up plan with the inverse actions; executing it reverts the state and writes the rollback to the audit chain.

A successful remediation is pinnable to a runbook. POST /api/v1/skills/{skillId}/runbook with the remediation ID persists a RunbookRegistryEntity. A later similar incident in the same tenant surfaces the runbook in the orchestrator's recall results.

After a successful investigation, the Hindsight bank for the tenant receives a write with the hypothesis + the resolved runbook (verifiable via `curl http://localhost:8888/health` returning 200 and the hindsight dashboard showing the new entry). A new investigation with a similar alert fingerprint reads from the bank and surfaces the prior runbook in the orchestrator's recall payload.

POST /api/v1/skills creates a per-tenant skill (runbook / escalation path). The skill is visible in GET /api/v1/skills for the same tenant and not for any other tenant. A new investigation that matches the skill's fingerprint is steered to invoke it (visible in the Langfuse trace as a system-prompt injection).

POST /api/v1/code-knowledge/repos with a repo URL and credentials indexes the repo: RepoNodeEntity, ServiceEdgeEntity and PackageDependencyEntity are written. GET /api/v1/code-knowledge/search?q=retry returns matching repos + lines, ranked by PatternEntity confidence.

During an investigation on a stack-trace alert, the code agent reads the indexed code and returns a "service=X, function=Y" answer that is consistent with RepoServiceMapEntity. The fix proposal references the actual file path; approving it opens a CodeCommit PR through the remediation flow.

POST /api/v1/integrations/slack/install completes the OAuth dance; the bot token + channel mapping are stored encrypted (KMS-encrypted ciphertext in SlackOAuthStateEntity / SlackNotificationEntity). A test event posted to /api/v1/integrations/slack/events with a valid signing secret triggers a chat reply; a tampered body returns 401.

With a Composio API key configured, GET /api/v1/integrations/composio/tools returns the list of available tools; POST /api/v1/integrations/composio/triggers with a trigger payload schedules a polling subscription (TriggerEntity). The trigger fires when the upstream source updates and the resulting event lands in the triage queue.

With the relay running in the customer sample network, a `psql` from the host against `order-postgres:5432` is refused (network isolated). The relay instead connects to `ws://localhost:3099/v1/relay/connect` and the investigation's DB analyst agent queries the order DB through the relay; the response is identical to a direct query.

Subscribe a browser to VAPID web push: POST /api/v1/notifications/subscribe with the endpoint + keys. On any incident severity change, the subscriber receives a push with the incident title and a deep link to /dashboard/incidents/:id. Revoking the subscription via DELETE removes the push record.

The widget bundle is loadable from /widget/widget.js (Vite build). Mounting the widget with a valid widget-session token renders a read-only incident panel; the panel shows the latest investigations for the tenant. Mounting with an invalid token renders an "unauthorized" state and never discloses incident data.

The dashboard SPA at /dashboard renders, on first load, a sidebar with the tenant's name, a list of recent incidents, and a "New investigation" button. Clicking an incident opens a detail view with the investigation's evidence, hypotheses, chat panel and a remediation panel; the panels stream live updates over SSE as the agents run.

A user action in the dashboard (e.g. approving a remediation) generates an OpenTelemetry span with a correlation id; the same id is searchable in Langfuse. With SENTRY_DSN set, an unhandled exception in a unit test scenario surfaces in Sentry's Issues view within 30 seconds.

The in-process scheduler (src/shared/infra/scheduler/scheduler.ts) runs the DLQ redrive job on its interval: a message manually pushed to a DLQ is re-driven to the source queue within one tick. The cost rollup job aggregates UsageRecordEntity into a per-tenant daily total and writes a UsageRecordEntity tagged type=daily_rollup.

Each investigation's total cost in src/shared/domain/cost.ts is computed from per-agent token counts; Langfuse publishes the same number to the metric recorder. A test run that exceeds the configured per-investigation ceiling is aborted and the incident is marked status=cost_exceeded, visible in /api/v1/investigation.

With a clean environment (no AWS_*, no STRIPE_*, no CLERK_*, no SENTRY_*, no LANGFUSE_*, no SVIX_*, no SLACK_*, no COMPOSIO_*, no MASTRA_* env vars set, only HINDSIGHT_* + optional ANTHROPIC_API_KEY + minimal app config), `docker compose up -d` starts `causeflow-postgres`, `redis`, `hindsight`, `causeflow-api` and `causeflow-worker`. Within 60 seconds `curl http://localhost:3099/health` returns 200 with JSON `{"postgres":"ok","redis":"ok","anthropic":"ok","queues":"ok"}` (Anthropic is "skipped" if no key is set). No AWS, Stripe, Clerk, Sentry, Langfuse, Svix, Slack, Composio, or Mastra endpoint is contacted at startup (verifiable with a packet capture or by grepping the worker boot log for outbound URLs).

Postgres is the only persistence layer. `psql` against the `causeflow-postgres` service lists 31 tables in the `causeflow` schema (one per existing entity). A `POST /api/v1/incidents` with an authenticated request writes a row that becomes visible in `SELECT * FROM causeflow.incidents WHERE tenant_id = $1`. The single-table ElectroDB code is removed (or kept behind a feature flag, off by default) — no `DynamoDBClient`, no `DocumentClient` instantiation at boot.

BullMQ on Redis replaces SQS. The boot log lists 4 BullMQ queues (`causeflow-alerts`, `causeflow-triage`, `causeflow-investigation`, `causeflow-remediation`) with their worker counts. `GET /admin/queues` (a new local-only admin endpoint) returns each queue's name, depth, completed/failed counts, and the last 5 jobs. A `POST /api/v1/incidents` enqueues a triage job, the worker picks it up within 2 seconds, the job moves to `completed`, and the incident row updates to status=triaged — without any SQS endpoint being called.

Local auth replaces Clerk. `POST /api/v1/auth/register` with `{ email, password, tenantName }` returns 201 with `{ user, tenant, token }` (a signed JWT, no external IdP). `POST /api/v1/auth/login` with the same credentials returns a fresh JWT; a wrong password returns 401. `GET /api/v1/whoami` with `Authorization: Bearer <token>` returns the user and tenant. The JWT is signed with `JWT_SECRET` from env, verified locally with `jose`, and the Clerk webhook endpoint, `clerk-client`, and `ClerkUserEmailResolver` are removed from the running code path. Tenant isolation (AC-010) still holds for tokens issued by this endpoint.

Stub billing replaces Stripe. `GET /api/v1/billing/subscription` returns `{"plan":"free","status":"active"}` for every tenant; `GET /api/v1/billing/usage` returns the tenant's local `UsageRecordEntity` rows paginated; `POST /api/v1/billing/checkout-session` returns 410 Gone with a clear "billing disabled in OSS build" message. No `stripe.com` call is made (verifiable by grepping the worker boot log for outbound URLs or by network capture). Quota enforcement (max parallel investigations, max agents per run) is enforced from env-supplied limits, not from a Stripe plan.

Token encryption uses AES-256-GCM from Node's `crypto`, keyed by `TOKEN_ENCRYPTION_KEY` env. A unit test encrypts a sample OAuth token, decrypts it, and confirms round-trip equality; tampering with one byte of the ciphertext fails the GCM auth tag check and raises. The `KmsTokenEncryption` class and the `alias/causeflow-token-encryption` provisioning step in `infra/localstack/init/01-create-resources.sh` are removed; no AWS KMS endpoint is ever called at startup.

The investigation worker runs as a `causeflow-worker` docker-compose service (same image as the API, different entrypoint). `docker compose ps` shows both `causeflow-api` and `causeflow-worker` healthy. An investigation enqueued in AC-041 is picked up by the worker (verifiable by tailing the worker container logs and confirming a `investigation:start` log line). No ECS task definition, no `EcsClient`, no AWS credentials are loaded — the `ecs/` directory is removed (or kept behind a feature flag, off by default).

Full local-only pipeline regression. `POST /api/v1/webhooks/datadog` with a valid HMAC creates an incident (replaces AC-014). A second identical POST within the dedup window does not create a duplicate (replaces AC-015). The triage worker picks it up, calls the Anthropic API, and sets severity (replaces AC-017). The investigation worker runs all 6+ agents and emits per-agent events on the SSE stream (replaces AC-019). The run completes, an EvidenceEntity and a HypothesisEntity are written to Postgres (replaces AC-020), and the runbook is written to the local Hindsight bank (replaces AC-026). The whole flow runs against the local-only stack with zero SaaS credentials set.

Remediation runs against the local stub cloud provider. `POST /api/v1/remediation/:id/approve` records an ApprovalEntity; `POST /api/v1/remediation/:id/execute` runs the plan through `StubCloudProvider`, which writes a deterministic mock result (success or failure based on the action type) and records each action's before/after in `RemediationEntity` (replaces AC-024). `POST /api/v1/remediation/:id/rollback` creates a follow-up plan with the inverse actions. No AWS API is contacted (verifiable via network capture); the `aws-cloud-provider.ts` and `azure-cloud-provider-stub.ts` are removed from the running code path.

RBAC and the audit chain work on the local-only stack. As a `viewer`, `GET /api/v1/audit` returns 200; `DELETE /api/v1/audit/:id` returns 403. As an `admin`, the same DELETE returns 200 and the audit chain advances (replaces AC-008). RBAC roles are read from the local `UserEntity.role` field, not from a Clerk org role claim.

Every optional SaaS integration is a no-op when its env var is empty. With `SENTRY_DSN=` empty, no Sentry SDK is initialised; an unhandled exception in a unit test scenario is logged to pino but never sent. With `LANGFUSE_*` empty, the tracer returns a no-op span and the metric recorder silently drops events. With `SVIX_*` empty, outbound webhooks are POSTed directly via `fetch` (no retry library). With `SLACK_*` empty, the chat platform port returns a "not configured" sentinel and Slack-bound messages degrade gracefully. With `COMPOSIO_API_KEY=` empty, the tool provider returns an empty tool list. With `MASTRA_ENABLED=false`, the Mastra agent runner is not even loaded. None of these integrations block startup or fail any acceptance check when empty.

`pnpm test:run` and `pnpm test:integration` pass with no AWS, Stripe or Clerk env vars set. The integration test stack runs against the same `causeflow-postgres` + `redis` + `hindsight` services (no ministack, no stripe-mock). `pnpm test:e2e` runs the full alert → triage → investigation → remediation flow end-to-end on the local-only stack. The total `aws-sdk-client-mock` dev dep is no longer used and can be removed; no test imports `@aws-sdk/*`, `stripe`, or `@clerk/backend`.

Port boundaries preserved — primary deliverable: I12 domain-purity invariant. The `domain/` layer has zero `pg`, `bullmq`, `stripe`, `@clerk/*`, or `@aws-sdk/*` imports (verified by `pnpm lint-invariants` I12 rule that greps the domain tree). The 17 port interfaces in `src/shared/application/ports/` are unchanged. The composition root at `src/bootstrap.ts` is the primary place concrete implementations are wired: `PostgresXxxRepository` (actual class names vary by module, e.g. `PgIncidentRepository`, `PgTenantRepository`), `BullMqMessageQueue`, `AesGcmTokenEncryption`, `StubCloudProvider`. Additional named implementations (`LocalAuthService`, `EnvCredentialVendor`, `LocalInvestigationTaskDispatcher`, `StubBillingService`) are deliverables of AC-042, AC-043, AC-045 respectively and are not part of this AC. The worker entrypoint at `src/workers/investigation-worker.ts` is a legitimate second wiring site for the background worker process — it independently wires Dynamo/PG repositories, cloud providers, credential vendors, message queues, and investigation use cases; this is an acceptable multi-entrypoint pattern per the project architecture.
Note: The `application/` layer currently imports infra-level modules in ~73 files across 11 modules (primarily `logger`, `IntegrationEntity`, `FeedbackEntity`, `getStripeClient`, `AWS_API_CALL_TOOL`, and similar infrastructure concerns). This is a known Clean Architecture violation — the dependency rule (Infra → Application → Domain) is not yet enforced for application/ → infra/ imports. A future invariant I14 should add this rule with a structured allowlist for the `logger` exception. This is tracked as technical debt; the current AC-051 is scoped to domain/ purity only (I12).

Hindsight is the one allowed external SaaS. `curl http://localhost:8888/health` returns 200. After a successful local-only investigation (AC-046), a write to the tenant's Hindsight bank is observable in the hindsight dashboard (or via `GET /v1/banks/<bankId>/memories`). A second similar incident in the same tenant surfaces the prior runbook in the orchestrator's recall payload (replaces AC-026's behaviour for the local build).

The bundled `docker-compose.yml` no longer references the `ministack`, `langfuse`, or `langfuse-worker` services; no `localstack-init` script runs. The `.env.example` ships with only `PORT`, `DATABASE_URL`, `REDIS_URL`, `JWT_SECRET`, `TOKEN_ENCRYPTION_KEY`, `HINDSIGHT_API_URL`, `HINDSIGHT_API_KEY`, and the optional `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` + the VAPID pair. Running `docker compose up -d` from a fresh clone without modifying `.env.example` brings the platform to a healthy `/health` in under 60 seconds and reproduces the local-only pipeline from AC-046.

tables.causeflow: - pk (string, partition key, tenant-scoped composite: e.g. TENANT#<tenantId>#<entityName>) - sk (string, sort key, e.g. <entityId> or <entityName>#<entityId>) - entityType (string, used to discriminate across 31 entity shapes) - tenantId (string, always present; used for cross-tenant guards) - GSI1PK / GSI1SK (status / time-based queries) - GSI2PK / GSI2SK (entity-shard queries for hot entities like EvidenceEntity) - GSI3PK / GSI3SK (cross-entity lookups e.g. incidentId → evidence / hypothesis / chat) - createdAt, updatedAt (ISO 8601 strings) - entity-payload (typed per entity)
tables.entities: - TenantEntity — tenant record + plan + region + clerkOrgId - UserEntity — user record (linked to Clerk user id) with role - UserSettingsEntity — per-user preferences (theme, timezone, notifications) - InviteEntity — pending email invite - ApiKeyEntity — issued API key (id, hash, scopes, lastUsedAt) - BillingAccountEntity — Stripe customer + subscription linkage - UsageRecordEntity — per-action metered usage - IncidentEntity — alert / incident record (status, severity, source, fingerprint, raw payload) - ApprovalEntity — human approval for a remediation - AuditEntryEntity — append-only audit log (hash chain: previousHash → hash) - ChatMessageEntity — investigation chat message - ChangeEventEntity — CloudTrail-style change event - EvidenceEntity — agent evidence (linked to investigationId) - FeedbackEntity — thumbs up/down on agent output - HypothesisEntity — investigation hypothesis (parent/child for the tree) - NotificationEntity — VAPID subscription + delivery state - OAuthTokenEntity — encrypted third-party OAuth tokens - PackageDependencyEntity — node in the customer's dep graph - PatternEntity — recurring code pattern with confidence - RemediationEntity — execution record (actions, before/after, status) - RepoNodeEntity — a repo in the customer's code index - RepoServiceMapEntity — service → repo mapping - RunbookRegistryEntity — pinned runbook for a fingerprint - ServiceEdgeEntity — service-to-service edge - ServiceNodeEntity — a service in the customer's service map - SlackNotificationEntity — Slack delivery record - SlackOAuthStateEntity — pending Slack OAuth state - ToolCallEntity — agent tool call (linked to investigationId + agent name) - TriggerEntity — Composio trigger subscription - IntegrationEntity — registered integration (webhook endpoints, secrets) - WidgetSessionEntity — widget session token

postgres_local_runtime.purpose: OSS local replacement for the single DynamoDB table. The 31 entities from the single-table design above are ported to one Postgres table per entity inside a `causeflow` schema in the bundled `causeflow-postgres:16-alpine` container. The repository interfaces in `domain/` are unchanged; only the `infra/` implementations swap from ElectroDB to a thin SQL layer (the `pg` Node driver, no ORM). Tenant isolation is enforced the same way (every table has `tenant_id NOT NULL`; repositories always pass the tenant id and never accept a raw `pk`/`sk`).

tables.causeflow_auth_users: - tenant_id (text, NOT NULL, FK→causeflow_tenants.id) - id (text, NOT NULL) - email (text, NOT NULL) - password_hash (text, bcrypt cost 12) - role (text, NOT NULL, CHECK in ('owner','admin','member','viewer')) - created_at, updated_at (timestamptz) - PRIMARY KEY (tenant_id, id) - UNIQUE (tenant_id, email)
tables.causeflow_auth_sessions: - jti (uuid, PK) - tenant_id (text, NOT NULL) - user_id (text, NOT NULL) - issued_at, expires_at (timestamptz) - revoked_at (timestamptz, NULL)
tables.causeflow_token_vault: - tenant_id (text, NOT NULL) - provider (text, NOT NULL) - ciphertext (bytea, NOT NULL) -- AES-256-GCM - iv (bytea, NOT NULL) - auth_tag (bytea, NOT NULL) - updated_at (timestamptz) - PRIMARY KEY (tenant_id, provider)
tables.causeflow_billing_stub: - tenant_id (text, PK) - plan (text, NOT NULL, default 'free') - status (text, NOT NULL, default 'active') - quota_max_parallel_investigations (int) - quota_max_agents_per_run (int) - updated_at (timestamptz)
tables.causeflow_usage_records: - id (uuid, PK) - tenant_id (text, NOT NULL) - investigation_id (text) - agent_name (text) - tokens_in (bigint) - tokens_out (bigint) - cost_usd (numeric(12,6)) - recorded_at (timestamptz)
tables.causeflow_remediation_stub_actions: - id (uuid, PK) - tenant_id (text, NOT NULL) - investigation_id (text) - action_type (text) - before_state (jsonb) - after_state (jsonb) - status (text, CHECK in ('succeeded','failed','skipped')) - executed_at (timestamptz)

postgres_local_runtime.notes: - The other 24 entity tables (tenants, users, api_keys, invites, incidents, evidence, hypotheses, hypotheses_tree, chat_messages, tool_calls, audit_entries, notifications, approvals, integrations, triggers, slack_oauth_state, slack_notifications, repo_nodes, service_edges, repo_service_maps, package_dependencies, patterns, runbook_registry, widget_sessions) follow the same shape: `tenant_id` + entity id PK, a JSONB `payload` column for the entity-specific data, plus typed columns where the domain entity has them. A new invariant I12 enforces that every table has `tenant_id NOT NULL`. - A `docker-entrypoint-initdb.d/01-schema.sql` script seeds the schema on first boot; the `causeflow` database is created by the postgres image's standard env. - `DatabaseUrl` is the single connection string (`postgres://causeflow:causeflow@causeflow-postgres:5432/causeflow`); `pg` is the only required driver.
