Auth & user - POST /api/v1/auth/login - GET /api/v1/whoami - POST /api/v1/users/invite - GET /api/v1/users - PATCH /api/v1/users/:id
Tenant & RBAC - GET /api/v1/tenants/:id - PATCH /api/v1/tenants/:id - POST /api/v1/api-keys - GET /api/v1/api-keys - DELETE /api/v1/api-keys/:id - POST /api/v1/beta-allowlist - GET /api/v1/beta-allowlist
Billing - POST /api/v1/billing/checkout-session - GET /api/v1/billing/subscription - POST /api/v1/billing/webhook - GET /api/v1/billing/usage - POST /api/v1/billing/portal
Ingestion - POST /api/v1/webhooks/datadog - POST /api/v1/webhooks/grafana - POST /api/v1/webhooks/cloudwatch - POST /api/v1/webhooks/sentry - POST /api/v1/incidents - GET /api/v1/incidents - GET /api/v1/incidents/:id - POST /api/v1/admin/incidents - GET /api/v1/admin/analytics
Triage - GET /api/v1/triage - GET /api/v1/triage/:id
Investigation - POST /api/v1/investigation - GET /api/v1/investigation - GET /api/v1/investigation/:id - GET /api/v1/investigation/:id/evidence - GET /api/v1/investigation/:id/hypotheses - POST /api/v1/investigation/:id/chat - GET /api/v1/investigation/:id/stream (SSE) - POST /api/v1/investigation/:id/cancel - POST /api/v1/investigation/:id/retry
Remediation - GET /api/v1/remediation/:id/proposal - POST /api/v1/remediation/:id/approve - POST /api/v1/remediation/:id/execute - POST /api/v1/remediation/:id/rollback
Memory & skills - GET /api/v1/memory - GET /api/v1/memory/:id - POST /api/v1/skills - GET /api/v1/skills - PATCH /api/v1/skills/:id - POST /api/v1/skills/:id/runbook
Code intelligence - POST /api/v1/code-knowledge/repos - GET /api/v1/code-knowledge/search - GET /api/v1/code-knowledge/service-map
Integration & notifications - POST /api/v1/integrations/slack/install - POST /api/v1/integrations/slack/events - GET /api/v1/integrations/composio/tools - POST /api/v1/integrations/composio/triggers - POST /api/v1/notifications/subscribe - DELETE /api/v1/notifications/subscribe - WS /v1/relay/connect
Widget & portal - POST /api/v1/widget/session - GET /api/v1/widget/incidents - GET /embed
Audit - GET /api/v1/audit - GET /api/v1/audit/:id
Health - GET /health - GET /ready
Static - GET /dashboard (Alpine.js + Tailwind SPA) - GET /widget/widget.js

ui_layout.main_structure: The product surface is a single Alpine.js + Tailwind SPA served by Hono at /dashboard. The shell has a fixed left sidebar (240px), a top bar (56px) with the tenant name, a "New investigation" CTA, and the user menu; the main panel fills the rest. Breakpoints: <768px collapses the sidebar to icons; <480px hides the sidebar entirely and uses a bottom-nav. The page background is a near-black (`bg-slate-950`); cards are slate-900 with a 1px slate-800 border; primary accent is a teal-400 / cyan-300 gradient. Typography is the system stack with Inter loaded from a CDN.
ui_layout.sidebar: Vertically stacked: brand mark + product name, list of investigations (filterable by status), Integrations, Settings, Audit Log. Items highlight on hover (slate-800) and selected (slate-700 + left teal border).
ui_layout.main_panels: Investigation list (table with columns: severity, title, source, status, age, owner) with a search box and a status filter. Clicking a row opens the detail view.
ui_layout.detail_view: Four stacked regions: (1) header with severity badge, title, status, timestamps; (2) live progress panel that consumes the SSE stream and shows per-agent cards updating in real time; (3) evidence + hypothesis tree on the left, chat on the right; (4) remediation panel (proposal → approve → execute → rollback) at the bottom.
ui_layout.modals: New investigation modal (manual alert form), invite-user modal, install-Slack modal (OAuth popup), settings modal (theme, notifications), confirm-approval modal (typed confirmation of the action).
ui_layout.embeddable_widget: Rendered into a host page via a single <script> tag. Compact card with the latest incidents (3 rows), a "view all" link, and a small status pill. Stays under 300px wide; respects the host's dark or light mode via prefers-color-scheme.

design_system.color_palette: Light mode (default for the embeddable widget): background #ffffff, surface #f8fafc, text #0f172a, border #e2e8f0, primary #0d9488 (teal-600), accent #06b6d4 (cyan-500), danger #dc2626. Dark mode (default for the dashboard): background #020617 (slate-950), surface #0f172a (slate-900), text #e2e8f0 (slate-200), border #1e293b (slate-800), primary #2dd4bf (teal-400), accent #67e8f9 (cyan-300), danger #f87171. Status colors: critical #b91c1c, high #ea580c, medium #ca8a04, low #16a34a, info #0284c7.
design_system.typography: System font stack with Inter as the primary face (loaded via Google Fonts CSS). Sizes: 12px (caption), 14px (body, default), 16px (lead), 20px (h3), 24px (h2), 32px (h1). Weights: 400 body, 500 emphasis, 600 headings, 700 numbers in metric cards. Mono: ui-monospace / SF Mono for IDs and code references.
design_system.components: Buttons: primary (teal-600 / teal-400), secondary (slate-700 outline), ghost (transparent, hover slate-800), danger (red-600). All buttons have a 6px vertical, 12px horizontal padding, 6px radius, and a 150ms ease-out background-color transition. Cards: 8px radius, 1px border (slate-800 dark / slate-200 light), 16px padding, no shadow in dark mode, 1-px shadow in light. Badges: severity (red/orange/yellow/green), status (slate-700 background, slate-100 text), 4px radius, uppercase, 11px font, 1px tracking. Inputs: 1px border, 6px radius, 8px padding, focus ring 2px teal-400 at 30% opacity. Tables: zebra rows (slate-900/950 in dark, slate-50/white in light), 12px row padding, 12px header.
design_system.animations: Sidebar item hover: 100ms background-color ease-out. Card appearance: 200ms fade-in + 4px translateY. SSE updates: the row flashes a 1px teal-400 outline for 600ms then fades. Buttons: 150ms ease-out on background-color. Modals: 200ms scale 0.95→1 + opacity 0→1. No spinners on long operations — use the live SSE progress panel.

key_interactions.alert_to_triage: A Datadog alert hits POST /api/v1/webhooks/datadog. The webhook-auth middleware verifies the HMAC, the parser normalises the payload into an IncidentEntity (status=received, source=datadog, fingerprint=hash of the alert body). The ingestion service enqueues a message to the triage SQS queue. The triage worker picks it up, calls the Anthropic API with ANTHROPIC_TRIAGE_MODEL (default claude-sonnet-4-6), and updates the incident with severity + suggested investigation mode. The status flips to triaged. The dashboard's incident list shows the new row in real time over SSE.
key_interactions.triage_to_investigation: The orchestrator pulls the triaged incident and starts an investigation run with at least 6 agents in parallel. Each agent reads evidence (logs, metrics, changes, code, infra, DB) and writes EvidenceEntity rows. The orchestrator deduplicates evidence, prunes low-confidence hypotheses, and may iterate. SSE events stream per-agent progress to the dashboard. The run completes with status=succeeded and the synthesis is persisted.
key_interactions.investigation_to_remediation: At the end of a successful investigation, the orchestrator proposes a remediation plan. The detail view shows the plan in the remediation panel. The user reviews each action, optionally edits the plan, and clicks "Approve". The approval is recorded in ApprovalEntity and the audit chain advances. The plan runs against the customer's AWS account through the cloud provider port. RemediationEntity records each action with status and a before/after diff. The user can click "Rollback" to create a follow-up plan with the inverse actions.
key_interactions.memory_write_and_recall: On remediation success, the resolved hypothesis + runbook is written to the tenant's Hindsight bank (memory write). On the next similar incident, the orchestrator queries the bank for the top-k similar runbooks (memory recall) and includes them in the system prompt so the agents start from a known-good runbook.
key_interactions.dashboard_navigation: The user lands on /dashboard, sees the investigation list, clicks a row to open the detail view. The detail view shows live progress (SSE), evidence, hypotheses, chat, and the remediation panel. The user can chat with the investigation, approve the remediation, or pin a successful runbook to the tenant's skills.
key_interactions.widget_embed: A tenant embeds the widget on their status page via <script src="https://app.causeflow.ai/widget/widget.js" data-tenant-id="..."></script>. The widget requests a session token from /api/v1/widget/session, then renders a read-only incident panel that polls /api/v1/widget/incidents every 30 seconds.

step.title: Foundation: local stack, table, queues, KMS, dashboard shell, health
step.tasks: - Run `docker-compose up -d` and confirm ministack / redis / langfuse / hindsight are healthy - Confirm `infra/localstack/init/01-create-resources.sh` has created the causeflow table, 4 queues + 4 DLQs, and the KMS alias - `pnpm dev`; confirm `curl http://localhost:3099/health` returns 200 with the four sub-checks - Open `http://localhost:3099/dashboard` and confirm the Alpine.js SPA shell renders - Run `pnpm lint-invariants`, `pnpm typecheck`, `pnpm lint`, `pnpm test:run`; all green

step.title: Tenancy & access: Clerk, tenant middleware, RBAC, audit chain
step.tasks: - Configure Clerk keys in `.env.localstack`; confirm POST /api/v1/auth/login returns a user + tenant - Implement and verify a cross-tenant guard (AC-010) - Confirm RBAC: viewer can read audit, admin can delete (AC-008) - Issue an API key, authenticate with it, revoke it (AC-009) - Confirm the audit hash chain advances on every state change (AC-008)

step.title: Billing: Stripe checkout, webhooks, metered usage
step.tasks: - Stripe test mode: create a checkout session, complete it via the CLI, confirm webhook (AC-011, AC-013) - Confirm a usage record is written on every investigation completion (AC-012) - Confirm quota enforcement (e.g. max parallel investigations) blocks over-quota tenants

step.title: Ingestion: Datadog / Grafana / CloudWatch / Sentry parsers, dedup, manual incident
step.tasks: - Send each of the four webhook shapes; confirm a row in IncidentEntity (AC-014, AC-016) - Confirm dedup within the window (AC-015) - Confirm HMAC rejection for tampered bodies (AC-014)

step.title: Triage: Anthropic severity classification
step.tasks: - Triage a real alert and confirm severity + suggested mode (AC-017) - Confirm a Langfuse trace exists with tenant + incident tags (AC-018) - Confirm model swap via env (AC-022)

step.title: Investigation: orchestrator + 6 agents, evidence, hypotheses, SSE, chat, registry
step.tasks: - Run a real investigation; confirm the SSE stream, evidence list, hypothesis tree (AC-019, AC-020) - Chat with the running investigation; confirm Slack mirror (AC-021) - Persist ToolCallEntity rows for each agent

step.title: Remediation: propose, approve, execute, rollback, pin runbook
step.tasks: - Approve a plan, execute it against the stub cloud provider, confirm RemediationEntity (AC-023, AC-024) - Roll back; confirm the audit chain records the inverse (AC-024) - Pin a successful remediation to a runbook; verify the next similar incident surfaces it (AC-025)

step.title: Memory & skills: Hindsight bank write + recall, per-tenant skills
step.tasks: - Confirm a write to the Hindsight bank on success and a recall on the next similar run (AC-026) - Create a per-tenant skill and confirm it steers a matching investigation (AC-027)

step.title: Code intelligence: repo index, code search, code agent → PR
step.tasks: - Index a repo; query the index; confirm matching code (AC-028) - Code agent proposes a patch; approval opens a CodeCommit PR (AC-029)

step.title: Integrations & notifications: GitHub App, Composio, Slack, Sentry, relay, VAPID
step.tasks: - Install Slack; trigger an event with a valid signing secret (AC-030) - Trigger a Composio tool + a Composio trigger (AC-031) - Verify the relay tunnel: a host psql is refused, the same query through the relay succeeds (AC-032) - Subscribe a browser to VAPID push; trigger an event; receive a push (AC-033)

step.title: Widget & portal: Vite build, embed, dashboard SPA
step.tasks: - Build the widget; mount it on a host page; verify with a valid + invalid session token (AC-034) - Click through the dashboard sidebar, list, detail view, modals (AC-035)

step.title: Observability & ops: OTel, Sentry, scheduler, cost ceiling
step.tasks: - Verify an OTel span + a Langfuse trace share a correlation id; verify Sentry receives an unhandled exception (AC-036) - Run the DLQ redrive + cost rollup jobs; verify outputs (AC-037) - Verify the per-investigation cost ceiling aborts an over-budget run (AC-038)

step.title: End-to-end smoke + deploy
step.tasks: - `pnpm test:e2e` against the docker-compose stack; full alert → triage → investigation → remediation flow - `pnpm test:smoke` against the deployed stack - `pnpm test:eval` (Promptfoo) for triage and pipeline quality - CDK synth + deploy dry-run via `pnpm lint-invariants` + `pnpm verify-deploy` - CI green on `.github/workflows/ci.yml`, `deploy.yml`, `eval.yml`

step.title: Open-source local runtime: replace AWS/Stripe/Clerk with self-hosted equivalents
step.tasks: - Drop `ministack`, `langfuse`, `langfuse-worker`, and `infra/localstack/init/01-create-resources.sh` from `docker-compose.yml`; add `causeflow-postgres:16-alpine` (with a `docker-entrypoint-initdb.d/01-schema.sql` that creates the `causeflow` schema) and a `causeflow-worker` service built from the same image - Port the 31 ElectroDB entities to one Postgres table per entity (composite PK `(tenant_id, id)`; typed columns + JSONB payload); delete `electrodb` from `package.json` and remove `src/shared/infra/db/entities/*`; add `pg` and a thin `src/shared/infra/db/postgres-client.ts` (AC-040) - Replace `SqsMessageQueue` with `BullMqMessageQueue`; add `GET /admin/queues` for Bull Board-style visibility; preserve retry + DLQ semantics (AC-041) - Replace Clerk: new `LocalAuthService` (bcrypt password hashing, JWT signing with `jose` + `JWT_SECRET`); new `POST /api/v1/auth/register` and `POST /api/v1/auth/login`; remove `clerk-client`, `handle-clerk-webhook`, `ClerkUserEmailResolver`, `/v1/auth/clerk-webhook` route; rewrite `auth.middleware.ts` to verify the local JWT (AC-042) - Replace Stripe: new `StubBillingService`; remove all `stripe.com` calls; `POST /api/v1/billing/checkout-session` returns 410 Gone; remove `STRIPE_*` from `.env.example`; keep `UsageRecordEntity` for metered usage (AC-043) - Replace STS: new `EnvCredentialVendor`; remove `sts-credential-vendor` and `role-policy-builder` (AC-039) - Replace KMS: new `AesGcmTokenEncryption` using Node's `crypto`; remove `kms-token-encryption` (AC-044) - Replace ECS: new `LocalInvestigationTaskDispatcher` that resolves to the bundled worker; remove `ecs-client`, `task-dispatcher` (AC-045) - Keep `StubCloudProvider` as the only cloud provider; remove `aws-cloud-provider`, `azure-cloud-provider-stub` (AC-047) - Make every optional SaaS no-op when its env var is empty: Sentry, Langfuse, Svix, Slack, Composio, Mastra (AC-049) - Add invariant I12: `src/modules/**/domain/**` and `src/shared/domain/**` must not import `pg`, `bullmq`, `stripe`, `@clerk/*`, or `@aws-sdk/*` (AC-051) - Update `infra/scripts/check-invariants.ts` to enforce I12; update `.github/workflows/ci.yml` to start the new docker-compose stack before running `pnpm test:integration` and `pnpm test:e2e` - Verify the full local-only pipeline (AC-046) and the 38 original checks still pass against the new stack

success_criteria.functionality: Every Acceptance Check AC-001 through AC-038 passes against the running docker-compose stack with no skips. The full path Datadog webhook → ingest → dedup → triage → investigation (6+ agents) → remediation proposal → approval → execution → rollback → memory write → recall-on-next-similar-incident is reproducible end-to-end via `pnpm test:e2e`. The audit chain advances on every state change and the tip is verifiable via `GET /api/v1/audit?limit=1`. RBAC blocks every state-changing endpoint for a viewer. Cross-tenant reads return 404. The per-investigation cost ceiling is enforced and the budget breach is visible in both the API and Langfuse. The relay tunnel is the only path from the control plane to a customer DB and a direct host connection to the customer DB is refused by the network.
success_criteria.user_experience: Dashboard SPA loads in under 1s on a warm cache. Live SSE progress appears on the detail view within 250ms of an agent emitting an event. The widget renders inside any host page without a flash of unstyled content. Approving a remediation is a typed-confirmation flow; the action the user is about to take is spelled out in plain English. Severity and status colors are readable in both dark and light mode. There are no spinners on long operations — the live SSE progress panel is the source of truth.
success_criteria.technical_quality: `pnpm typecheck` is green. `pnpm lint` is green. `pnpm lint-invariants` is green (I1–I11 enforced, no cross-module direct calls, no infra-in-domain, no hard-coded secrets). `pnpm test:run` runs 174 unit tests in under 15 seconds. `pnpm test:integration` runs 6 integration tests against the docker-compose stack. `pnpm test:e2e` runs 6 E2E tests. Domain layer has zero external imports. Application layer never imports infra. The composition root at `src/bootstrap.ts` is the only place concrete implementations are wired. The relay (`core/relay`) is a separate git submodule registered in `.harness/projects.json` and is not part of `core`'s Work Items.
success_criteria.design_polish: All colors come from the documented palette. All animations respect `prefers-reduced-motion`. The embeddable widget renders cleanly inside a 300px column on a third-party status page. The dashboard renders cleanly at 1920×1080, 1366×768, 768×1024 and 375×667. Severity and status badges are always uppercase, always with a 1px tracking, always with the palette color, never inline-styled. No emoji in production UI text.
success_criteria.open_source_local_runtime: Acceptance Checks AC-039 through AC-053 all pass against the running docker-compose stack with no AWS, Stripe, Clerk, Sentry, Langfuse, Svix, Slack, Composio, or Mastra env vars configured. `docker compose up -d` from a fresh clone with an unchanged `.env.example` brings the platform to `/health=200` in under 60 seconds, and the full Datadog webhook → ingest → dedup → triage → investigation → remediation → memory pipeline (AC-046) is reproducible on the local-only stack. The 38 original acceptance checks (AC-001..AC-038) continue to pass on the new stack; port boundaries and invariants I1–I12 are enforced.
